name: Tennessee — Safety, Corrections & Transportation
tagline: A grouping of Tennessee's public-safety and transportation agencies — Safety & Homeland Security (driver services, Highway Patrol), Correction, and Transportation (TDOT) — large field, edge, data, and intelligent-transportation footprints whose IT is delivered largely through the centralized STS organization.
overview: This account groups three of Tennessee's largest operational agencies, all headquartered in Nashville and all carrying heavy field, edge, and data footprints. The Department of Safety & Homeland Security runs driver services (license/ID issuance, REAL ID, self-service kiosks, mobile units) and the Tennessee Highway Patrol, plus the state's homeland-security and fusion functions. The Department of Correction (TDOC) operates the state prison system — roughly 20,000 in custody — and is building a Nashville intelligence center, deploying drones against contraband, and weighing body-worn cameras. The Department of Transportation (TDOT) runs the highway program and a sizable intelligent-transportation-systems (ITS) estate — TDOT SmartWay, the I-24 SMART Corridor with AI-based decision support, and a Memphis–Blue Oval City smart-freight pilot. Crucially, IT for these agencies is delivered largely by Strategic Technology Solutions (STS), the centralized IT organization under Finance & Administration, which sets standards, security, and cloud policy; agency-specific systems (biometrics, ITS, corrections intelligence) are procured on top of that shared platform. For a Dell SLG seller, the demand is concentrated in edge/mobile devices, ruggedized field compute, data-center and storage for records and video, AI/analytics for traffic and corrections intelligence, and the networking behind ITS — most of it routed through the state's Central Procurement Office Statewide Contracts and shaped upstream by STS.

budget.intro: These agencies are funded through Tennessee's annual (July 1–June 30) budget; the FY2027 budget Gov. Lee signed in May 2026 totals roughly $58B. There is no consolidated 'state IT' line — technology is distributed across agencies and the centralized STS organization, so the relevant signals are agency program budgets plus STS-driven modernization. TDOT received $400M in one-time FY2027 infrastructure funds on top of its ~$1.2B annual work program and the Transportation Modernization Act; TDOC requested roughly $1.5B with discrete technology lines.

stats.label: FY2027 state budget
stats.note: Signed May 2026; the pool agency and CPO-vehicle buys draw from. FY begins July 1, 2026

stats.label: TDOT FY2027 infrastructure
stats.note: One-time funds: ~$50M maintenance, $150M bridge preservation, $200M capacity-adding projects

stats.label: TDOC tech lines (FY2026–27)
stats.note: Per budget hearing: ~$8.6M technology upgrades plus ~$1.7M drone/contraband program

notes: No consolidated state IT budget line exists; confirm each agency's technology budget and the STS operating budget when forecasting, as STS delivers much of the underlying infrastructure.

TDOT's capital is dominated by the $15B fiscally constrained 10-Year Plan and the Transportation Modernization Act ($3B state general-fund transfer); ITS and technology spend is a subset of the broader program.
Figures are from public budget hearings and announcements and should be confirmed against final appropriations and agency LARs before forecasting.

initiatives.name: TDOT ITS — I-24 SMART Corridor & AI decision support
initiatives.summary: TDOT operates TDOT SmartWay and the I-24 SMART Corridor, a ~28-mile integrated corridor (Nashville–Murfreesboro) with VSL gantries, cameras, and an AI-based decision-support system developed with Southwest Research Institute and Vanderbilt. Phase 2 completed in early 2025; Phase 3 adds ramp metering, fiber, CCTV, and dynamic message signs — sustained demand for edge compute, networking, storage, and AI/analytics.

initiatives.name: TDOT smart-freight & connected-autonomous pilots
initiatives.summary: TDOT named a private partner (Cavnue) for a Memphis–Blue Oval City Smart Freight Corridor pilot on I-40 and is piloting connected/autonomous freight technology in real-world conditions — a multi-year program with roadside infrastructure, data, and analytics needs adjacent to the West Tennessee EV manufacturing buildout.

initiatives.name: TDOC corrections intelligence, drones & body cameras
initiatives.summary: The Department of Correction is standing up a Nashville central intelligence center (~30 staff) using AI to monitor inmate activity, plans ~$1.7M in drones to interdict contraband, and faces legislative pressure to pilot body-worn cameras at a private facility — opportunities across analytics compute, storage/retention for video, and edge devices, with public-records and retention constraints to design around.

initiatives.name: Driver services modernization (Safety)
initiatives.summary: Safety & Homeland Security runs license/ID issuance with self-service kiosks and mobile units, REAL ID, and a new noncitizen credential rollout beginning Jan 2026. Tennessee has not broadly launched a mobile/digital driver license — a likely future modernization track around secure issuance and identity infrastructure.

procurement.intro: These agencies buy through Tennessee's centralized machinery, not standalone agency RFPs in most cases. The primary path is the Central Procurement Office's Statewide Contracts (with a dedicated technology category) and the Edison supplier portal; larger or specialized systems (ITS integration, biometrics, corrections platforms) can run as agency solicitations. STS sets the IT standards, cloud, and security policy that buys must conform to.

vehicles.name: CPO Statewide Contracts (SWC)
vehicles.detail: The primary vehicle — pre-competed technology agreements state agencies (and many local/nonprofit buyers) use without their own RFP. Confirm Dell's SWC coverage and reseller scope are current for the categories these agencies buy.

vehicles.name: STS-delivered shared services
vehicles.detail: STS provides the centralized infrastructure, cloud, and security platform these agencies run on — engage STS for enterprise infrastructure and modernization demand that underpins agency systems.

vehicles.name: Edison Supplier Portal
vehicles.detail: Mandatory supplier registration and the transactional backbone for doing business with the state — ensure Dell and partners are correctly registered before pursuing awards.

vehicles.name: Agency / cooperative solicitations
vehicles.detail: Specialized systems (ITS integration, biometrics, corrections intelligence) may run as agency RFPs or via cooperative contracts (e.g., NASPO ValuePoint) — track agency and Central Procurement postings and verify TN adoption for cooperative terms.

powerStructure.name: Jeff Long
powerStructure.title: Commissioner, Safety & Homeland Security
powerStructure.note: Appointed by Gov. Lee in 2019; oversees driver services, the Tennessee Highway Patrol, and homeland-security functions. The top relationship for the Safety/THP portion of the account.

powerStructure.name: Frank Strada
powerStructure.title: Commissioner, Dept of Correction (TDOC)
powerStructure.note: Appointed January 2023; leads the state prison system (~20,000 in custody) and the technology agenda around the new intelligence center, drones, and body-camera debate. The top relationship for the corrections portion.

powerStructure.name: Will Reid
powerStructure.title: Commissioner, Dept of Transportation (TDOT)
powerStructure.note: Former TDOT chief engineer elevated to Commissioner; owns the highway program, the 10-Year Plan, and the ITS/SMART Corridor and smart-freight initiatives. The top relationship for the transportation portion.

powerStructure.name: Kristin Darby
powerStructure.title: State CIO, Strategic Technology Solutions (F&A)
powerStructure.note: Took office March 2025; leads the centralized STS org that delivers IT for these agencies, co-chairs the State AI and Cybersecurity Councils, and describes a pragmatic cloud-first posture that keeps some low-latency/regulated workloads on-prem. The strategic relationship across all three agencies.

powerStructure.name: Central Procurement Office (DGS)
powerStructure.title: Statewide procurement authority
powerStructure.note: Within the Department of General Services; competes and awards the Statewide Contracts and runs Edison registration that gate most technology buys for these agencies.

news.title: FY2027 budget dedicates $400M to TDOT infrastructure
news.impact: One-time funds for maintenance, bridge preservation, and capacity projects expand TDOT's program — supporting context for ITS, fiber, and roadside-technology demand that procures through statewide vehicles.

news.title: TDOT names partner for Memphis–Blue Oval City smart-freight / autonomous pilot
news.impact: Launches a multi-year connected/autonomous freight program on I-40 — roadside infrastructure, data, and analytics needs adjacent to the West Tennessee manufacturing buildout.

news.title: TDOC budget hearing details ~$8.6M tech upgrades and ~$1.7M drone/contraband program
news.impact: Concrete corrections technology spend plus a Nashville AI intelligence center — entry points for analytics compute, storage/retention, and edge devices.

news.title: Senate panel advances body-worn-camera pilot for TN prison guards
news.impact: Signals a likely corrections video-capture and retention program — position storage, data management, and edge devices, accounting for public-records and retention constraints.

news.title: TDOT completes Phase 2 of the I-24 SMART Corridor
news.impact: Advances the AI-based integrated-corridor-management deployment; Phase 3 (ramp metering, fiber, CCTV, DMS) is an opening for edge compute, networking, storage, and analytics.

dellActions: Confirm Dell's CPO Statewide Contract coverage and reseller scope for the categories these agencies buy — it is the shared access path across Safety, Correction, and TDOT, not a per-agency vehicle.

Engage STS (CIO Kristin Darby) as the centralized delivery layer for enterprise infrastructure, cloud, and security, then map agency-specific demand (ITS, corrections intelligence, biometrics) on top of that platform.

Lead with edge and field infrastructure for TDOT ITS (SMART Corridor, smart-freight, roadside compute/networking) and AI/analytics for traffic decision support, positioning ahead of I-24 Phase 3 and the I-40 freight pilot.

Target TDOC's intelligence center, drone program, and probable body-camera rollout with analytics compute and high-capacity, governed storage/retention — design for public-records and retention constraints.

Watch driver-services modernization at Safety (kiosks, mobile units, new credential rollout, a probable future mobile/digital DL) and position secure issuance and identity infrastructure ahead of any program.
